mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rate levels relative to trailing 12-month average keep the discount rate applied to equity cash flows compressed, supporting an upward drift in S&P 500 prices over a quarterly horizon. This is a continuous, always-on carry effect not contingent on a specific crossing event.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.16% as of 2004-12, well below long-run historical average
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 60-day forward SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year walk-forward window, or periods of low FEDFUNDS show no statistically significant outperformance versus periods of high FEDFUNDS (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate declines by more than 0.2 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window, it signals tightening labor markets and strengthening consumer income, which supports risk asset demand and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.2pp over trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.4% as of Dec 2004, down from cycle peak
failure_condition: Among triggered instances (3-month UNRATE decline > 0.2pp), 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in fewer than 55% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts combined with a low federal funds rate jointly signal a self-reinforcing credit-fueled demand expansion: cheap financing (low FEDFUNDS) amplifies the positive wealth and construction-employment effects of rising HOUST, producing above-trend S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon. Neither series alone captures this amplification.
candidate_trigger: HOUST above 1700k units AND FEDFUNDS below 3%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1771k with FEDFUNDS at 2.16% as of late 2004
regime_hint: monetary easing combined with housing boom
failure_condition: Interaction term (HOUST level x inverse FEDFUNDS) fails to add incremental Rank IC beyond either series individually (delta IC < 0.02) over any 3-year rolling window, or the joint-trigger regime underperforms the unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained positive month-over-month trend in Industrial Production Index reflects expanding real output capacity and corporate earnings momentum, which continuously supports S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon regardless of specific threshold levels.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 117.813 as of Nov 2004, trending upward
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing INDPRO growth rate and 20-day forward SPX return is below 0.03 across any rolling 3-year window, or the sign of the correlation flips persistently for more than 12 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly nonfarm payrolls print that surprises materially above the trailing 6-month average growth rate triggers a short-lived risk-on rally in equities over the following trading week, driven by upward revisions to growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: monthly PAYEMS change exceeds trailing 6-month average change by more than 50,000 jobs
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 132,266 thousand as of Dec 2004
failure_condition: In triggered months, 5-day forward SPX returns are positive in fewer than 55% of instances, or the effect fails to reach significance (p > 0.10) over a 5-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-inflation environment, robust retail sales growth (RSAFS) signals healthy consumer spending that translates into corporate revenue beats and supports S&P 500 gains over a one-month horizon. In high-inflation regimes, the same retail sales strength instead raises fears of monetary tightening and the relationship weakens or reverses.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 349,436 million and CPIAUCSL at 191.2 as of late 2004, low-inflation environment
failure_condition: The positive relationship between RSAFS growth and 20-day forward returns fails to hold (Rank IC < 0.03) specifically during periods when CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation is below 3%, invalidating the regime-conditional claim.

mechanism_narrative: During periods where the federal funds rate is below the trailing year-over-year CPI inflation rate (negative real policy rate), equity markets receive sustained support from cheap real financing costs, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon. When real rates turn positive, this support mechanism reverses or disappears.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.16% versus CPI inflation implying a negative real policy rate as of late 2004
failure_condition: During periods of negative real FEDFUNDS (FEDFUNDS minus trailing YoY CPIAUCSL inflation < 0), 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly higher than during positive real rate periods (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint deterioration in labor conditions, defined as UNRATE rising by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months while PAYEMS month-over-month growth simultaneously turns negative, signals an incipient recession (Sahm-rule-like dynamic) and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month as markets price in earnings downgrades. Neither indicator alone is sufficient to trigger this signal.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over 3 months AND PAYEMS monthly change is negative
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.4% and PAYEMS growing as of late 2004, no current trigger active
failure_condition: Among triggered instances, 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in fewer than 55% of cases, or the joint-trigger signal's Rank IC is not meaningfully higher (delta < 0.02) than using UNRATE or PAYEMS individually over any 3-year rolling window.
